steps.title: Quick question first…
steps.question: Magnetic field lines around a bar magnet travel…
options: From south pole to north pole outside the magnet
From north pole to south pole outside the magnet
Randomly in all directions with no pattern
Only along the surface of the magnet
steps.misconceptionFeedback: ⚠️ Common misconception! By convention, magnetic field lines run from the north pole to the south pole outside the magnet. They form closed loops that continue south to north inside the magnet.
steps.correctFeedback: ✓ Correct! Field lines run from north to south outside the magnet. They are closest together (most dense) at the poles, where the field is strongest.
steps.bestSource: BEST resource: AQA Physics specification 4.7.1 — Magnetic fields

steps.title: Magnetic Fields
steps.bodyHTML: A magnetic field is a region where a magnetic material or moving charge experiences a force. Field lines run from the north pole to the south pole outside the magnet, and the strength of the field is shown by how close together the lines are — the denser the lines, the stronger the field. Field lines never cross each other.
commonErrors: Drawing field lines from south to north (they go N to S outside the magnet)
Making field lines cross each other
Thinking a uniform field means no force acts

aqa.questionText: Describe the magnetic field pattern around a bar magnet.

steps.label: State the direction of field lines
steps.content: Field lines run from the north pole to the south pole outside the magnet.
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — correct direction: north to south outside

steps.label: Describe field strength
steps.content: The field is strongest at the poles where the field lines are closest together (most dense) and weakest further away where lines are more spread out.
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — reference to density of lines / strongest at poles

steps.label: Describe the shape
steps.content: The field lines form closed loops — they continue from south to north inside the magnet.
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — closed loops / continuous through magnet

aqa.finalAnswer: Field lines run from N to S outside; densest at poles (strongest field); closed loops continuing S to N inside the magnet.

aqa.questionText: Where is the magnetic field of a bar magnet strongest?

options.label: A
options.text: In the middle of the magnet

options.label: B
options.text: At the poles, where field lines are closest together

options.label: C
options.text: At the poles, where field lines are furthest apart

options.label: D
options.text: Equally strong everywhere around the magnet

markSchemeSteps: Strongest at poles — field lines are most dense (closest together) there ✓
aqa.commonMistake: Option C reverses the rule — dense lines mean strong field, not weak.
